name: Xiao Long Tang Bao
recipeIngredient: 11 ounces Pork loin with some fat, chopped or ground coarsely
recipeInstructions:
1. The Chinese name for this dish can be transliterated as "Xiao Long Tang Bao" or "Hsiao Lung T'ang Pao."
2. Mix the first eight ingredients together.
3. Divide the mixture into 24 portions.
4. Cut the aspic into 24 portions.
5. Knead the dough for about 5 minutes and roll it out into a thin baguette shape.
6. Cut the dough into 24 pieces, then flatten each piece into a round measuring approximately 2 inches in diameter.
7. Squeeze the edges of each round with your fingers to make them very thin, resulting in a medium-thin center and a thin outside.
8. Place ½ ounce (1 portion) of filling in the center of each round.
9. Top each piece with one portion of aspic.
10. Fold up the sides of each round and twist in a decorative pattern, ensuring the top is sealed tightly.
11. If any holes appear, patch them immediately.
12. Place each dumpling on a square of waxed paper or a piece of bok choy leaf.
13. Steam the dumplings, preferably in a bamboo steamer, for 8 minutes. Serve hot.
Note on aspic:
If you don't have aspic available, prepare homemade chicken broth by boiling about 1 cup of broth (or good-quality canned low-salt broth) uncovered for approximately 2 minutes, reducing it slightly. Dissolve 1 envelope gelatin in the remaining ½ cup and stir over low heat until fully dissolved. Chill the mixture. This will yield enough aspic for three recipes of this dim sum dish.
